task_completion.description: Open the noisy image in ImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ/Fiji application is open displaying the 'noisy_blobs.tif' image.
The 'Bandpass Filter' dialog in ImageJ is visible. 'Filter large structures down to' is set to 40, 'Filter small structures up to' is set to 3, 4, or 5, and 'Suppress stripes' is set to 'None'.
A 'Save As' dialog or file manager/terminal shows the filtered image being saved to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/filtered_image.tif'.

task_completion.description: Measure image statistics before and after filtering
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ 'Results' window is visible showing 'Mean' and 'StdDev' measurements for the image.

task_completion.description: Create the CSV report with statistics
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the creation or content of 'bandpass_filter_results.csv', including columns 'Condition', 'Mean', and 'StdDev', and rows for before and after filtering.

integrity.description: Agent used ImageJ GUI for filtering and measuring
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with ImageJ menus (e.g., Process > FFT > Bandpass Filter, Analyze > Measure) rather than running a pre-written script to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: CSV report contains genuine measured data
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered into the CSV match the measurements shown in the ImageJ Results window, rather than being arbitrary or placeholder numbers.

integrity.description: Agent did not cheat by copying the ground truth image
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent actually applies the filter in ImageJ and saves the result, rather than copying '/var/lib/imagej_ground_truth/clean_reference.tif' to the output directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is located at ~/ImageJ_Data/raw/noisy_blobs.tif. The FFT Bandpass Filter should be configured with large structures ~40 pixels and small structures ~3-5 pixels. The filtered image must be saved to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/filtered_image.tif. The CSV report must be saved to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/bandpass_filter_results.csv with columns 'Condition', 'Mean', and 'StdDev'. The synthetic noisy image has a Mean of ~38.32 and StdDev of ~45.76. Success requires at least a 10% reduction in standard deviation while preserving the mean intensity within 20% tolerance.
